edges.description: Lys650Met increases receptor tyrosine kinase activity.
Constitutive FGFR3 activity disrupts endochondral bone growth.
edges.target: Severe disturbances in endochondral bone growth

nodes.description: The Lys650Met mutation causes increased constitutive FGFR3 kinase activity.

biological_processes: fibroblast growth factor receptor signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: A novel skeletal dysplasia with developmental delay and acanthosis nigricans is caused by a Lys650Met mutation in the fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 gene.
evidence.snippet: Transient transfection studies with FGFR3 mutant constructs show that the Lys650Met mutation causes a dramatic increase in constitutive receptor kinase activity, approximately three times greater than that observed with the Lys650Glu mutation.

evidence.reference_title: Suppression of severe achondroplasia with developmental delay and acanthosis nigricans by the p.Thr651Pro mutation.
evidence.snippet: The disorder is the result of a single missense mutation at codon 650 (p.Lys650Met) in the fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 gene (FGFR3).

evidence.reference_title: FGFR3 / severe achondroplasia-developmental delay-acanthosis nigricans syndrome (Moderate)
evidence.snippet: FGFR3 | HGNC:3690 | severe achondroplasia-developmental delay-acanthosis nigricans syndrome | MONDO:0014658 | AD | Moderate

nodes.description: A single missense mutation at codon 650 (p.Lys650Met) in FGFR3 causes SADDAN.

evidence.reference_title: Suppression of severe achondroplasia with developmental delay and acanthosis nigricans by the p.Thr651Pro mutation.
evidence.snippet: The disorder is the result of a single missense mutation at codon 650 (p.Lys650Met) in the fibroblast growth factor receptor 3 gene (FGFR3).

nodes.description: The Lys650Met mutation results in severe disturbances in endochondral bone growth.
nodes.id: Severe disturbances in endochondral bone growth

evidence.reference_title: Severe achondroplasia with developmental delay and acanthosis nigricans (SADDAN): phenotypic analysis of a new skeletal dysplasia caused by a Lys650Met mutation in fibroblast growth factor receptor 3.
evidence.snippet: The FGFR3 Lys650Met mutation results in severe disturbances in endochondral bone growth that approach and overlap those observed in thanatophoric dysplasia, type I.
